SEARCH
Package client.view

Class BaseController

java.lang.Object
client.view.BaseController
Direct Known Subclasses:
ApprovalQueueController, CancellationsReportController, GateController, GuideRegisterController, NotificationCenterController, ParkParamsController, ReservationCreateController, ReservationListController, SubscriberRegisterController, VisitsReportController, WaitlistController
public abstract class BaseController extends Object
Lifecycle base for screens that participate in the realtime push channel.

Subclasses get four hooks:

  • subscribe(String, long, Consumer) — register interest in a server-side entity by id; sends a fire-and-forget SUBSCRIBE to the server and attaches a local EventBus callback. The EventBus.Subscription handle is retained so it can be detached automatically.
  • onShow() — non-final hook fired when the screen becomes active; default no-op. Subscribe here when the data the screen needs is known at activation time. Many screens subscribe later (after the user types an id), in which case they call subscribe(java.lang.String, long, java.util.function.Consumer<common.dto.ServerEvent>) from their own callbacks instead.
  • onHide() — final; called by Navigator just before this screen is swapped out. Sends UNSUBSCRIBE for every active subscription, detaches the local callbacks, and then invokes onHideHook(). Final on purpose so subclasses cannot forget the unsubscribe step.
  • onHideHook() — overridable template for additional cleanup (close auxiliary streams, cancel timers, etc.) without breaking the unsubscribe contract.

Fire-and-forget SUBSCRIBE caveat. subscribe(java.lang.String, long, java.util.function.Consumer<common.dto.ServerEvent>) does not block on the server's reply. Between the SUBSCRIBE going out and the registry acknowledging it, an event published by another client could still slip past us. For a college-project demo this race is acceptable — the worst case is missing the first event after a freshly opened screen.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    final void
    onHide()
    Called by Navigator just before this screen is swapped out.
    void
    onShow()
    Called by Navigator after a fresh screen has been loaded and its dependencies wired.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • onShow

      public void onShow()
      Called by Navigator after a fresh screen has been loaded and its dependencies wired. Default implementation is a no-op; subclasses override to subscribe when they know what to subscribe to at activation time.

    • onHide

      public final void onHide()
      Called by Navigator just before this screen is swapped out. Drains every active subscription, then invokes onHideHook() for subclass-specific cleanup. Final on purpose.
